title: customTemplateTypes activation parity — how a value-space directive registry reaches upgrade parity with the behavior registry across the plugged/unplugged boot models, and where a per-registry insertion cascade must be generalized
summary: Prep research for decision #2010 (parity-wiring seam under the #1994 for-each migration epic; we:reports/2026-07-01-2010-customtemplatetypes-parity-wiring.md). The migrated for-each becomes a typed `<template type="for-each">` on the minted `CustomTemplateTypeRegistry`, but that registry is instantiated NOWHERE in the app today (only unit tests), so dropping for-each's `CustomAttribute` define without wiring `customTemplateTypes` activation silently and gate-invisibly breaks live for-each. The question: how does `customTemplateTypes.upgrade` reach parity with the `window.attributes` (CustomAttribute) upgrade path across two boot models — plugged (fui:plugs/bootstrap.ts, per-consumer upgrade, no global cascade) and unplugged (fui:plugs/bootstrapUnplugged.ts, unified register()+upgrade cascade). CODE GROUND TRUTH (frontierui, verified 2026-07-01): the item's premise drifted materially. (1) The '~15 plugged sites' is WRONG — the real surface is 4 eager app/demo `.upgrade(document.body)` sites (fui:demos/loan-origination/app.ts:660-661, fui:demos/auto-insurance/app.ts:739-740, fui:demos/lazy-traits-plugged.ts:41, fui:demos/inert-dead-zone.ts:41) + 3 detached-fragment cascade sites in ONE file (fui:plugs/webexpressions/ExplicitHTMLInsertion.patch.ts:88-89,102-103,117-130) = 7. (2) The item's claim that the insertion cascade / InjectorRoot drive `window.attributes.upgrade` is FALSE — ExplicitHTMLInsertion resolves ONLY `customTextNodes` (hardcoded, fui:plugs/webexpressions/ExplicitHTMLInsertion.patch.ts:52-54), and InjectorRoot.upgrade (fui:plugs/webinjectors/InjectorRoot.ts:247) manages the injector CHAIN, not value-space registries; the nav blocks have ZERO upgrade calls. (3) KEY MECHANISM: both CustomAttributeRegistry.upgrade and CustomTemplateTypeRegistry.upgrade install a MutationObserver with childList:true/subtree:true and upgrade addedNodes on any later connected-tree insert (fui:plugs/webbehaviors/CustomAttributeRegistry.ts:512-514,560-562; fui:plugs/webdirectives/CustomTemplateTypeRegistry.ts #createObserver) — so once upgrade(root) runs, subsequent connected-tree inserts are handled by the registry's own observer with NO per-site wiring; only DETACHED fragments (createContextualFragment/setHTMLUnsafe, upgraded before connection) need the explicit cascade. F1 — plugged registration is settled-by-in-code-precedent: `window.attributes` is a BARE global (fui:plugs/bootstrap.ts:196, needs per-site upgrade), while 6 value-space registries ride the injector chain via documentInjector.set(...) (fui:plugs/bootstrap.ts:209-258); customTemplateTypes belongs with the EAGER-WALK class (attributes), not the lazily-resolved chain class, because it re-prototypes templates + observes. F2 — the 'register on the chain → inherit the ExplicitHTMLInsertion cascade for free' idea is FALSE: resolveRegistry hardcodes 'customTextNodes' (fui:plugs/webexpressions/ExplicitHTMLInsertion.patch.ts:54), so cascade coverage for the sibling directive registry must be added explicitly — the ONE genuine sub-choice left is: generalize the shared cascade resolver to also upgrade the directive registry, vs duplicate a resolve+upgrade per branch. F3 — unplugged parity is a genuine one-liner `register(customTemplateTypes)` (fui:plugs/bootstrapUnplugged.ts:216-219, alongside register(attributes)); value-space chain registries there use docInjector.set (fui:plugs/bootstrapUnplugged.ts:160-186), but customTemplateTypes needs the eager walk register() provides, matching attributes. F4 — how other custom-element frameworks batch registry upgrades: Vue app.directive()/Angular/Alpine register+match by an element discriminator at runtime and rely on a single mount/hydration walk plus a MutationObserver-equivalent for dynamic inserts (vuejs.org/guide/reusability/custom-directives) — WE's per-registry-observer-owns-its-tree model mirrors that (each registry self-observes rather than a global scheduler batching all upgrades), which is why parity is 'call upgrade(root) once per activation site' not 'hook a global upgrade bus'. SYNTHESIS for #2010: this collapses to NOT a ratifiable merit fork — the plugged registration is mechanical follow-the-precedent (register customTemplateTypes on the injector chain + eager upgrade at the same 4 sites attributes uses), unplugged is a one-line register(), and candidate (c) (coupling CustomAttributeRegistry.upgrade to the sibling) is foreclosed in code by #1986 rule-1 (registration-home separation, fui:plugs/webdirectives/CustomTemplateTypeRegistry.ts:15-18). The lone surviving sub-choice is the ExplicitHTMLInsertion cascade generalization (F2). Codifies no new statute (--codified-to=one-off); the nearest anchor (we:docs/agent/platform-decisions.md:2222, #1983/#1987 <template type> value grammar) governs values, not activation wiring.
